name: Balconies of Picos de Europa Loop from Cangas de Onís
name_original: Balcones de Picos de Europa – circular desde Cangas de Onís
description: Cycle 51.3 miles (82.6 km) on this difficult road cycling route through the Picos de Europa, gaining 6281 feet (1914 metres).
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Parres are characterized by the dramatic terrain of the Cantabrian Mountains and the Sierra del Sueve. The region features significant river valleys, including the Piloña and Sella, which offer varied cycling experiences. Cyclists can expect demanding ascents to viewpoints like Mirador del Fito, situated at 578 meters, alongside routes through picturesque Asturian landscapes. This area provides a compelling mix of challenging climbs and scenic rides, often leading into the broader Picos de Europa region. Best road cycling routes around Parres The most popular road cycling route is Balconies of Picos de Europa Loop from Cangas de Onís, a 51.3 miles (82.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 45 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views of the Picos de Europa. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Queen's Viewpoint – Lagos de Covadonga loop from Cueves, a difficult 49.6 miles (79.9 km) path. This route features significant climbs towards the notable Lagos de Covadonga and scenic viewpoints. Local road cyclists also love the Arriondas – Collada Llomena loop from Arriondas, a 50.9 miles (82.0 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 50 minutes. Road cycling around Parres is defined by dramatic mountain ranges, lush river valleys, and challenging ascents to panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Parres are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 24 reviews. More than 2000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Parres's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Parres?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Parres, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a diverse selection, from moderate rides to very challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Parres?
A: Road cycling in Parres is characterized by the dramatic terrain of the Cantabrian Mountains and the Sierra del Sueve. You can expect a compelling mix of challenging climbs, such as the ascent to Mirador del Fito, and scenic rides through lush river valleys like the Piloña and Sella. The region often leads into the broader Picos de Europa area, offering extensive mountain experiences.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Parres?
A: The region around Parres offers excellent road cycling opportunities for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. While specific seasonal considerations can vary, the diverse terrain supports a wide range of microclimates, making it a great destination outside of peak winter conditions.

Q: Are there options for less experienced road cyclists in Parres?
A: Yes, Parres offers some routes suitable for less experienced road cyclists. While many routes feature significant climbs, there are at least 2 easy routes and 8 moderate routes available, providing options for those looking for a less demanding ride.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Parres is renowned for its challenging terrain, with 12 difficult routes available. Experienced riders can tackle demanding ascents like the one from Lloroñi to Mirador del Fito, or routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1078480886/balconies-of-picos-de-europa-loop-from-cangas-de-onis" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balconies of Picos de Europa Loop from Cangas de Onís&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 1900 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Parres?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Parres are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24634612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queen's Viewpoint – Lagos de Covadonga loop from Cueves&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20500166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arriondas – Collada Llomena loop from Arriondas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What notable landmarks or viewpoints can I see while road cycling in Parres?
A: Road cycling routes in Parres offer access to stunning landmarks and viewpoints. You can cycle to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6247078" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Fito&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the Asturian coastline and the Picos de Europa. Other routes pass near the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/492581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Covadonga&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4501879" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basilica of Santa María la Real de Covadonga&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, the region around Parres is rich in cultural and historical sites. You can cycle towards the significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/492581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Covadonga&lt;/a&gt;, a major pilgrimage site. The nearby town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1127146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribadesella&lt;/a&gt;, known for its adventure sports, is also accessible, as seen in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40339822" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arriondas – Ribadesella Town Center loop from Arriondas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I cycle to the famous Lagos de Covadonga?
A: Yes, you can cycle towards the notable Lagos de Covadonga. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24634612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queen's Viewpoint – Lagos de Covadonga loop from Cueves&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that features significant climbs towards this iconic destination and its scenic viewpoints.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Parres?
A: The road cycling routes in Parres are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 24 reviews. More than 2000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Parres's varied terrain, often praising the dramatic mountain ranges, lush river valleys, and challenging ascents to panoramic viewpoints.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that offer extensive views of the Picos de Europa?
A: Many routes in Parres provide breathtaking views of the Picos de Europa. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1078480886/balconies-of-picos-de-europa-loop-from-cangas-de-onis" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balconies of Picos de Europa Loop from Cangas de Onís&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to offer extensive views of this stunning national park.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: While specific amenities vary by route, the region around Parres includes charming towns like Cangas de Onís and Arriondas, which serve as starting points or pass-throughs for many routes. These towns, along with others in the area, typically offer cafes and other establishments where cyclists can stop for refreshments.
tips: Incredible climb with the coast on your right to end on a wonderful balcony to the Picos de Europa, the views are incredible and the descent to Riocaliente is brutal if you like winding and narrow roads
Beautiful views of the bridge that crosses the Sella River in Ribadesella

On one of the most famous hills in the Sueve mountain range stands an old concrete watchtower from the early 20th century: the Mirador del Fito. From here, we can capture a seamless, almost blink-and-you-miss sequence shot that immediately reveals the basic elements of the Asturian landscape: sea, mid-mountains, high mountains, meadows, valleys, fog, and towns.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Balcones de Picos de Europa road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is categorized as a "Hard road ride" and requires "Very good fitness". With approximately 82.6 kilometers and a significant elevation gain of 1,910 meters, it's a challenging ride best suited for experienced cyclists.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: Cyclists generally complete this route in about 4 hours and 46 minutes, though this can vary based on individual fitness and stops along the way.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The majority of the route consists of asphalt (77.0 km) and paved tracks (5.65 km), with sections of road and national road. While generally featuring mostly well-paved surfaces, some demanding segments might include unpaved sections or require dismounting and pushing your bike.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers spectacular views. You'll pass by the challenging climb of Alto del Torno via Nueva, cross the scenic Bridge over the Sella River, and enjoy expansive vistas from Mirador del Fito, which offers views of both the coastline and the Picos de Europa.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this route in Cangas de Onís?
faqs.answer: Cangas de Onís serves as the gateway town for this circular route. You should be able to find parking options within the town itself, which is a common starting point for exploring the Picos de Europa.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Balcones de Picos de Europa?
faqs.answer: The Picos de Europa region can experience varied weather. For the most enjoyable experience, consider cycling during late spring, summer, or early autumn when conditions are generally more stable and temperatures are milder. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: As this route is primarily on public roads within the Picos de Europa National Park, you typically do not need special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle it. However, always respect park regulations and local guidelines.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the Picos de Europa National Park, this specific route is a challenging road cycling tour. It's not typically suitable for dogs to accompany cyclists due to the distance, elevation, and traffic on the roads. If you plan to bring a dog, consider shorter, dedicated walking trails in the area.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll be treated to the spectacular landscapes of the Picos de Europa, renowned for its jagged limestone peaks, dramatic gorges, and incomparable scenery. The route offers lush green valleys and breathtaking vistas throughout, including coastal views from higher points.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling or hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly shares sections with several other official trails, including the [CIMA AS24] El Fito * Les Arriondes, Mtb Northern Route (Asturia), Northern Route (Asturia), and [CIMA AS24] El Fito * Loroñe, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay in Cangas de Onís after the ride?
faqs.answer: Cangas de Onís is a charming mountain village and a popular base for visitors to the Picos de Europa. It offers superb local food, traditional cider, and various accommodation options, making it an excellent place to relax and refuel after your challenging ride.
